title: Psalms 62
subtitle: Truly My Soul Finds Rest in God

sections.header: Verses 1–6 — “He Alone Is My Rock and My Salvation”

heb.paragraph: "Truly (ʾak̅) my soul finds rest in God; my salvation comes from him. Truly he is my rock and my salvation; he is my fortress, I will never be shaken" (vv.1–2). The word ʾak̅ (truly/only) appears six times in the psalm — the keyword of exclusivity. ONLY God is rock, salvation, fortress. The refrain repeats (vv.1–2, 5–6) with one change: "never be shaken" (v.2) becomes "I will not be shaken" (v.6). The confidence deepens.

notes.note: "Truly my soul finds rest in God. He alone is my rock." The soul’s rest is not in resolution of circumstances but in the character of God. Six times "truly/only" (ʾak̅) emphasises exclusivity.
"How long will you assault me? You are all like a leaning wall, a tottering fence." The enemies are structurally unsound — they look standing but are about to collapse.
"Yes, my soul, find rest in God; my hope comes from him." David preaches to his own soul (as in Ps 42–43). Self-counsel: direct your soul toward God.

heb.paragraph: "One thing God has spoken, two things I have heard: ‘Power (ʿōz) belongs to God’ and ‘with you, Lord, is unfailing love’" (vv.11–12). The psalm’s theological summit: God has BOTH power and love. Power without love is tyranny; love without power is impotence. God possesses both simultaneously. David warns: do not trust in extortion or stolen wealth (v.10). If riches increase, do not set your heart on them (v.10). Trust God alone.

notes.note: "Trust in him at all times, you people; pour out your hearts before him. God is our refuge." The invitation: all times, all people, all hearts poured out. Prayer is not selective; it is constant.

"Surely the lowborn are but a breath, the highborn a delusion. On a balance they go up; they are together lighter than a breath." Both social classes weigh nothing. And: do not trust in extortion or riches.
"Power belongs to God, and with you, Lord, is unfailing love." The two-things declaration: absolute power + absolute love. This combination exists only in God.

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVYes, my soul, find rest in God; my hope comes from him.ESVFor God alone, O my soul, wait in silence, for my hope is from him.NIVSurely they intend to topple me from my lofty place; they take delight in lies. With their mouths they bless, but in their hearts they curse.ESVThey only plan to thrust him down from his high position. They take pleasure in falsehood. They bless with their mouths, but inwardly they curse. Selah

rec.text: ""Truly my soul finds rest in God. He alone is my rock." The soul’s rest is not in resolution of circumstances but in the character of God. Six times "truly/only" (ʾak̅) emphasises exclusivity."

rows.label: Power and Love — 62:7–12
rows.text: "Power belongs to God AND unfailing love." Both classes lighter than breath. Trust at all times.

hebtext.note: "Truly (ʾak̅) my soul finds rest in God; my salvation comes from him. Truly he is my rock and my salvation; he is my fortress, I will never be shaken" (vv.1–2). The word ʾak̅ (truly/only) appears six times in the psalm — the keyword of exclusivity. ONLY God is rock, salvation, fortress. The refrain repeats (vv.1–2, 5–6) with one change: "never be shaken" (v.2) becomes "I will not be shaken" (v.6). The confidence deepens.

"One thing God has spoken, two things I have heard: ‘Power (ʿōz) belongs to God’ and ‘with you, Lord, is unfailing love’" (vv.11–12). The psalm’s theological summit: God has BOTH power and love. Power without love is tyranny; love without power is impotence. God possesses both simultaneously. David warns: do not trust in extortion or stolen wealth (v.10). If riches increase, do not set your heart on them (v.10). Trust God alone.

themes.note: Faith peaks at 10: the psalm of exclusive trust — six times "truly/only." Mercy: power AND love. Sovereignty: both belong to God alone.
